Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Enum SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Identifies specific Apple IIe soft switches for type-safe access.

public enum SoftSwitches.SoftSwitchId

Fields

AltChar = 7

ALTCHAR switch ($C00E/$C00F). Selects alternate character set for text mode.

AltZp = 4

ALTZP switch ($C008/$C009). When enabled, zero page ($0000-$01FF) and stack ($0100-$01FF) are mapped to auxiliary memory.

An0 = 12

Annunciator 0 ($C058/$C059). General-purpose output bit, often used for peripheral control.

An1 = 13

Annunciator 1 ($C05A/$C05B). General-purpose output bit, often used for peripheral control.

An2 = 14

Annunciator 2 ($C05C/$C05D). General-purpose output bit, often used for peripheral control.

An3 = 15

Annunciator 3 ($C05E/$C05F). General-purpose output bit, often used for peripheral control (commonly used for double hi-res mode).

Bank1 = 16

BANK1 switch. Selects which bank of auxiliary memory is active for the language card area ($D000-$FFFF).

HiRes = 11

HIRES switch ($C056/$C057). When enabled, selects high-resolution graphics mode; when disabled, selects low-resolution graphics mode.

HighRead = 18

HIGHREAD switch. Controls whether reads from $D000-$FFFF come from RAM or ROM.

HighWrite = 17

HIGHWRITE switch. Controls write protection for the language card RAM in the $D000-$FFFF range.

IntCxRom = 3

INTCXROM switch ($C006/$C007). When enabled, accesses to $C100-$CFFF use internal ROM instead of peripheral card ROMs.

Mixed = 9

MIXED switch ($C052/$C053). When enabled, displays graphics with 4 lines of text at the bottom of the screen.

Page2 = 10

PAGE2 switch ($C054/$C055). Selects display page (page 1 or page 2) and may affect auxiliary memory access depending on 80STORE state.

PreWrite = 19

PREWRITE switch. Pre-write state for language card write protection. Two consecutive reads of certain addresses are required to enable writing.

RamRd = 1

RAMRD switch ($C002/$C003). Controls whether reads from certain memory ranges come from main or auxiliary memory.

RamWrt = 2

RAMWRT switch ($C004/$C005). Controls whether writes to certain memory ranges go to main or auxiliary memory.

SlotC3Rom = 5

SLOTC3ROM switch ($C00A/$C00B). When enabled, accesses to $C300-$C3FF use internal ROM instead of slot 3 card ROM.

Store80 = 0

80STORE switch ($C000/$C001). When enabled, affects whether PAGE2 switch controls auxiliary memory or video display page selection.

Text = 8

TEXT switch ($C050/$C051). When enabled, display shows text mode; when disabled, shows graphics mode.

Vid80 = 6

80VID switch ($C00C/$C00D). Enables 80-column video mode when set.

Remarks

Each enum value corresponds to a specific Apple IIe soft switch that controls memory mapping, video modes, ROM selection, or annunciators.
